name: White, Jacobs & Associates
description_short: Attorney-managed credit repair firm based in Plano, TX. Disputes negative items using an in-house attorney and assigns each client a personal credit analyst.
description_long:
White Jacobs & Associates is a credit repair firm headquartered in Plano, Texas, operating under the tagline "The Credit Authority™." The company markets itself as a national provider, claiming "restoration across the nation," though its founding year, BBB accreditation status, and formal certifications such as NFCC or HUD approval are not publicly disclosed on its website. Its core distinction is the involvement of an in-house attorney in the dispute process — a structural choice that sets it apart from the majority of standard credit repair operations staffed only by analysts or paralegals.

The company's primary offering is attorney-managed credit repair, in which an in-house attorney oversees client disputes and audits across all three major credit bureaus — Equifax, TransUnion, and Experian. Target items include collections, charge-offs, late payments, repossessions, student loans, bankruptcies, foreclosures, and hard inquiries. Beyond dispute work, the firm provides mortgage approval support aimed at helping mortgage brokers close deals with clients who don't yet qualify due to credit issues. They also offer debt settlement services and claim dispute code removal typically within 72 hours. Notably, they assist clients in adding positive credit lines alongside removing negatives — a more holistic approach than dispute-only firms. Pricing is not published on the website; no monthly fees, setup costs, or plan tiers are disclosed publicly.

White Jacobs & Associates differentiates itself through the escalating nature of its process: rounds 1 and 2 involve standard bureau disputes, while rounds 3 and 4 escalate to direct attorney-managed audits targeting creditors and bureaus that haven't responded. Each client is assigned a dedicated personal credit analyst and a customized strategy — not a cookie-cutter dispute template. The company explicitly criticizes subscription-model competitors for lacking financial urgency to complete cases quickly. Client-reported results on their website show meaningful score improvements: one example shows a client moving from mid-500s to high-600s and low-700s across all three bureaus.

The firm's strongest assets are its attorney involvement, high Google rating (4.7 out of 5 from 942 reviews), and the breadth of services that extend into mortgage support and debt settlement. The main limitation is transparency: pricing, guarantees, BBB status, and certifications are absent from the public website. Consumers should expect this to be a premium-priced service relative to DIY or non-attorney credit repair options. It is not a non-profit, does not offer free counseling, and is not HUD- or NFCC-affiliated — consumers seeking low-cost or government-backed assistance should look elsewhere.

tiers.name: Credit Repair Program
features: Credit report analysis across all three bureaus
Dispute filing for inaccurate and unverifiable items

pros: In-house attorney actively manages the dispute and audit process — legal oversight most credit repair competitors don't offer
Escalating dispute strategy: rounds 3 and 4 involve direct attorney-managed audits targeting non-responsive bureaus and creditors
Each client is assigned a dedicated personal credit analyst for 1-on-1 service
Addresses all three major bureaus (Equifax, TransUnion, Experian) simultaneously
Goes beyond dispute removal — also helps clients add positive credit lines to rebuild credit proactively
Strong Google reputation: 4.7 out of 5 stars from 942 reviews
Serves mortgage brokers directly, offering a referral pipeline for clients who need credit work before qualifying for a loan
cons: Pricing is entirely opaque — no fees, monthly costs, or plan tiers are published on the website
No money-back guarantee mentioned anywhere on the site
BBB rating and accreditation status are unverified — cannot confirm standing with the Better Business Bureau
Not a non-profit and holds no NFCC, HUD, or CDFI credentials — not a low-cost or government-backed option
No confirmed online portal or mobile app for tracking dispute progress
best_for: Consumers with multiple serious derogatory items — bankruptcies, foreclosures, repossessions, or large collections — who need legal-grade dispute leverage
Home buyers or refinancers needing credit score improvement before qualifying for a mortgage
Individuals who have already tried standard credit repair and need escalation to attorney-managed audits
Mortgage brokers looking for a referral partner to help borderline clients become loan-eligible
diagnosis: White Jacobs & Associates is best suited for consumers with significant credit damage who want professional, attorney-backed dispute representation and are willing to pay a premium for it. The main caveat is full pricing opacity — prospective clients must contact the firm directly to understand costs, and no guarantee or refund policy is publicly available to reduce financial risk. Consumers comparing credit repair companies should also consider whether credit monitoring services, secured credit cards, or credit counseling might address their needs alongside or instead of paid credit repair services.
services: Attorney-managed credit dispute and audit process
Personal credit analyst assigned to each client
Multi-round escalating dispute strategy (up to 4 rounds)
Bureau disputes across Equifax, TransUnion, and Experian
Removal of collections, charge-offs, and late payments
Removal of bankruptcies, foreclosures, and repossessions
Mortgage approval support for brokers and their clients
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
